issues.sample: I NOTICE A BURNING OIL ODOR FROM INSIDE MY TRAX AFTER THE CAR WARMS UP OR IN WARM WEATHER. USING THE AIR CONDITIONER IS OUT OF THE QUESTION ON THESE 90+ DEGREE DAYS. I PLAN TO GET THE CAR IN TOMMORROW TO HE CHECKED. BETWEEN THE BURNING OIL UNDER 9500 MILES, THE BAD BRAKES UNDER 4

( FIRST COMPLAINT)WHEN IN GEAR ON A HILL THE VEHICLE STARTS ROLLING BACK WARD FOR NO REASON I NOW HAVE TO DRIVE WITH TWO FEET  (SECOND COMPLAINT) MY REAR VIEW CAMERA YOU CAN ONLY SEE 3/4 OF THE PICTUER THE PART IS BLACKED OUT.  (THIRD COMPLAINT) THE OIL THINS VERY FAST HAD HTREE

TL* THE CONTACT OWNS A 2015 CHEVROLET TRAX. THE CONTACT STATED THAT WHILE ON AN INCLINE WITH THE BRAKES ENGAGED, THE VEHICLE ROLLED BACKWARDS WITHOUT ANY WARNING. THE VEHICLE WAS TAKEN TO THE DEALER. THE TECHNICIAN DIAGNOSED THAT THE FAILURE WAS DUE TO A SLEEP MODE PROGRAM IN THE

I HAVE HAD MY TRAX IN FOR SERVICE 5+ TIMES DUE TO THE "BYOM" MEDIA AND DUE TO MY CAR BEING HACKED THROUGH THE "GLITCH" THROUGH THE MYLINK MEDIA CENTER AND INFORMANT SYSTEM, WHICH, IRONICALLY, AT THE BEGINNING OF MAY 2016, A RECALL NOTICE WAS SENT OUT ABOUT THIS ISSUE. I HAD MY CA

TL* THE CONTACT OWNED A 2015 CHEVROLET TRAX. WHILE DRIVING APPROXIMATELY 35 MPH, THE CONTACT CRASHED INTO THE REAR OF THE PRECEDING VEHICLE. THE AIR BAGS DID NOT DEPLOY. THE CONTACT SUSTAINED INJURIES THAT REQUIRED MEDICAL ATTENTION. A POLICE REPORT WAS FILED. THE VEHICLE WAS TOW

narrative.headline: What 2015 Chevrolet Trax owners report most
narrative.summary: Across 94 owner complaints to NHTSA, the most reported area is Engine (23% of reports). Some reports involve a fire or crash — read the details below.
